title: Add immersive 3D match view with Three.js (React Three Fiber)

requirements.summary: Create a new CricketStadium3D component using React Three Fiber that renders an immersive 3D cricket stadium scene with pitch, outfield, boundary, stadium stands, crowd atmosphere, and broadcast-style camera angle
acceptanceCriteria: A 3D canvas renders a recognizable cricket ground with pitch strip, outfield, and boundary
Stadium stands/crowd geometry is visible around the boundary
Lighting gives a floodlit stadium atmosphere
Camera angle is elevated broadcast/isometric style looking down the pitch
Component renders without errors on all match pages

file_operations.description: Add @react-three/fiber, @react-three/drei, and three as dependencies to support 3D rendering capabilities in React

Create the main 3D stadium component that renders a Canvas from @react-three/fiber containing: a flat green pitch strip mesh in the center, an outfield ground plane with boundary rope marker (circular or elliptical), basic stadium stands geometry (extruded shapes or simple meshes representing crowd tiers) positioned around the boundary, ambient lighting and directional lighting configured to provide warm floodlight-style glow, and a PerspectiveCamera positioned above and behind the batsman end looking down the pitch toward the bowler end at an elevated broadcast angle. Accept props for match state to drive dynamic updates in child components.

requirements.summary: Add animated ball delivery trajectory to CricketStadium3D with parabolic arc animation, stumps-hit effects on wickets, and boundary-clearing animation on sixes
acceptanceCriteria: Ball animates from bowler end to batsman end for each delivery
Animation follows a parabolic arc
Animation completes within 1.5 seconds
Wicket deliveries show a stumps-scatter effect
Six deliveries show ball trajectory continuing over the boundary

file_operations.description: Add a Cricket Ball component (small sphere mesh) that animates along a parabolic trajectory from bowler end to batsman end when a new ball event prop is received. Use React Three Fiber's useFrame hook or react-spring/three animation library to interpolate ball position over 1-1.5 seconds. Set ball color (red/white) based on match format prop. On wicket events, trigger a stumps-scatter particle effect or animated mesh. On six events, extend the ball trajectory to continue over the boundary rope before resetting.

requirements.summary: Add 11 fielder position markers that dynamically reposition based on match situation (attacking vs defensive field)
acceptanceCriteria: 11 fielder markers are visible on the 3D ground at all times
Fielder positions shift between attacking and defensive configurations based on match state
Markers are clearly distinguishable from the pitch/ground
Wicketkeeper position is always behind the stumps at batsman end

file_operations.description: Add 11 fielder marker meshes (small colored cylinders or capsules) positioned on the ground plane. Define two field configuration presets: attacking (more slips, gully, close-in fielders) and defensive (spread outfield positions). Accept match state props (wickets fallen, overs bowled, run rate) and compute which configuration to use based on match situation logic. Use smooth transitions when repositioning fielders. Ensure wicketkeeper marker is always placed behind the stumps at the batsman end.

requirements.summary: Integrate CricketStadium3D into InternationalMatch.tsx above the existing scorecard and commentary feed, ensuring ball animations sync with match simulation and responsive layout on mobile
acceptanceCriteria: 3D stadium view appears above the scorecard on the InternationalMatch screen
Ball animation triggers on each 'Next Ball' and during Auto-Simulate
Existing scorecard, commentary feed, and match logic remain fully functional
Layout is responsive — 3D view adapts to smaller screens
No regression in match result summary or navigation

file_operations.description: Import and render the CricketStadium3D component in the upper portion of the match screen (50-60% height) above the existing Scorecard and CommentaryFeed components. Pass current ball event, match state (wickets, overs, runs, format), and team data as props to CricketStadium3D. Ensure ball animation triggers are wired to the 'Next Ball' button handler and the auto-simulate interval logic. Add responsive styling so the 3D view collapses or reduces height on mobile/small screens. Verify that existing match simulation logic, backend calls, scorecard rendering, commentary feed, and result summary screen remain fully functional with no regressions.

requirements.summary: Add stadium atmosphere effects including crowd wave particles on six/wicket events and floodlight cone effects for night-match ambiance
acceptanceCriteria: Floodlight cone effects are visible from corner towers
Crowd wave particle effect triggers on six and wicket events
Visual effects do not cause noticeable frame rate degradation
Atmosphere effects are consistent with the dark/floodlit cricket theme

file_operations.description: Add four floodlight tower meshes positioned at the stadium corners, each emitting semi-transparent cone-shaped light shaft meshes (using MeshBasicMaterial with low opacity) to create a night-match floodlit atmosphere. Add a particle system or animated sprite elements on the stadium stands that trigger a crowd wave ripple effect (colored particles moving in a wave pattern) when six or wicket events occur. Optimize particle count and mesh complexity to maintain 30fps or better on average hardware. Ensure visual effects enhance the dark/floodlit cricket stadium theme without overwhelming the core game view.
